title: End Of Evangelion Trading Cards
wikitext:
[]: End Of Evangelion Trading Cards             Quote Post by Amitch991 » Thu Jul 07, 2022 11:32 am Being that the 25th anniversary of EoE is coming up very shortly, i've been browsing Ebay for some EoE related merch from the time of release. I found some listings for End of Evangelion trading cards that the seller claims were produced by SEGA and Bandai from 1997. I attach a link to one of the listings for reference : https://www.ebay.co.uk/itm/255583695315 I have not heard about these trading cards before, and i am thinking of buying a few of them from the seller. I'm wondering if anyone here has more information on them and could possibly confirm the legitimacy of these cards. Thanks in Advance

[]: Re: End Of Evangelion Trading Cards             Quote Post by silvermoonlight » Thu Jul 07, 2022 1:07 pm I own these trading cards they come in 2 sizes, and they are the real deal. Both are rare and the small size is really hard to get hold off, most of the time you'll only come across the extra wide.

[]: Re: End Of Evangelion Trading Cards             Quote Post by Amitch991 » Fri Jul 08, 2022 1:43 am silvermoonlight wrote: I own these trading cards they come in 2 sizes, and they are the real deal. Both are rare and the small size is really hard to get hold off, most of the time you'll only come across the extra wide.

[]: Re: End Of Evangelion Trading Cards             Quote Post by MuscleRobo » Wed Jul 20, 2022 2:51 pm I believe the description on the back of one of the cards was the best answer we had for a while about why Rei doesn't transform for Aoba in EoE, right?

[]: Re: End Of Evangelion Trading Cards             Quote Post by hamstercactus » Sat Sep 03, 2022 3:11 am silvermoonlight wrote: I own these trading cards they come in 2 sizes, and they are the real deal. Both are rare and the small size is really hard to get hold off, most of the time you'll only come across the extra wide.

[]: Re: End Of Evangelion Trading Cards             Quote Post by silvermoonlight » Sat Sep 03, 2022 10:22 am hamstercactus wrote: I've been looking at those boxes but can't find any information about the contents. I would assume it comes with all the cards from that series but want to make sure before I commit to buying it. Is it missing anything, as far as rare or special cards?

[]: Re: End Of Evangelion Trading Cards             Quote Post by hamstercactus » Sun Sep 04, 2022 4:28 am Oh, that makes sense. What would you say is a decent price then? I see an option to buy one case for $120 but now knowing it's a bunch of unopened packs, the price seems doubled what it should be. There's a couple single packs on ebay for around $10 each too.

[]: Re: End Of Evangelion Trading Cards             Quote Post by silvermoonlight » Sun Sep 04, 2022 10:12 am hamstercactus wrote: Oh, that makes sense. What would you say is a decent price then? I see an option to buy one case for $120 but now knowing it's a bunch of unopened packs, the price seems doubled what it should be. There's a couple single packs on ebay for around $10 each too.

[]: Re: End Of Evangelion Trading Cards             Quote Post by deeRez » Mon Sep 05, 2022 12:05 am From my experience, the ebay sellers from Japan really jack up those prices. Buy from yahoo auctions instead using a proxy. $10 CDs I bought from Japan are going for $70 on ebay. No thanks.

[]: Re: End Of Evangelion Trading Cards             Quote Post by hamstercactus » Mon Sep 05, 2022 6:38 am silvermoonlight wrote: I'd search for an unopened pack or a seller who is selling off the whole set and buy from a Japanese seller not a US seller...
